channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Strain UK Fish and Chip Industry Amid Global Conflicts
item.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East has significantly impacted Britain's fish and chip sector, causing a surge in fuel and energy prices. Peter Bruce, a fisherman from Peterhead in Scotland, has seen d...

item.title: Arusha youth develops smart glasses to support people with visual impairments
item.description: A 19-year-old student from Radio Habari Maalum College in Arusha, Majaliwa Tanu, has designed an innovative pair of smart eyeglasses aimed at improving mobility for people with visual impairments. The...

item.title: China warns the Philippines over maritime activities amid West Philippine Sea tension
item.description: On April 5, 2026, China’s Embassy in Manila urged the Philippine government to immediately stop what it described as 'maritime infringement and provocation' in the West Philippine Sea (WPS). Embassy s...

item.title: Weather alert: Rain, strong winds, and snowfall expected across Pakistan
item.description: According to the National Emergencies Operation Centre (NEOC) under NDMA, Pakistan is likely to experience rain, thunderstorms, strong winds, and even snowfall over the next 12 to 24 hours due to a we...

item.title: Understanding Pluralism Through Personal Experiences During Sri Lanka’s Civil War
item.description: The article reflects on growing up in Sri Lanka during the civil war and the lessons it offers on pluralism. Eranda Jayawickreme recalls life in Kandy, a city of rich religious diversity, where Buddhi...

item.title: Australia braces for lasting economic and fuel price impacts from Middle East conflict
item.description: The Australian government has warned that the economic and fuel price impacts of the ongoing war in the Middle East will persist well after the conflict ends. Assistant Foreign Affairs Minister Matt T...

item.title: Saskatchewan Court upholds cryptocurrency exchange in major fraud ruling
item.description: The Saskatchewan Court of Appeal has ruled in favour of a cryptocurrency exchange in a fraud dispute involving $240,000, marking an important precedent for digital financial services in Canada. The ca...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Leads Easter Sunday Mass and Delivers Urbi et Orbi Blessing at the Vatican
item.description: On April 5, 2026, Pope Leo XIV celebrated Easter Sunday Mass at Saint Peter's Square in the Vatican, marking the conclusion of his first Holy Week as the pontiff. The Mass commemorated the resurrectio...

item.title: Josh Papali'i returns as Canberra Raiders seek vital win against the Knights
item.description: The Canberra Raiders are under pressure to secure a much-needed victory as they face the Newcastle Knights, hoping to break a tough three-game losing streak that’s seen them tumble toward the bottom o...

item.title: CAN urges Nigerian leaders to take concrete actions this Easter for national revival
item.description: For dis Easter period, the Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN) don give clear message say Nigeria fit rise again if those wey dey lead make correct and visible moves. Archbishop Daniel Okoh, wey be...

item.title: Colin Jost Comments on Trump and Pam Bondi’s Handling of Epstein Files on SNL
item.description: During a recent 'Weekend Update' segment on Saturday Night Live, host Colin Jost humorously addressed former President Donald Trump's decision to fire Attorney General Pam Bondi. Bondi’s dismissal rep...

item.title: Storm Dave causes travel disruption and high winds across the UK
item.description: Storm Dave brought severe winds and travel disruption across the United Kingdom before easing on Easter Sunday. The Met Office lifted amber and yellow warnings early on Sunday after the strongest gust...

item.title: Daily Challenges and Care for Children with Type 1 Diabetes in the Philippines
item.description: Type 1 diabetes (T1D) is a chronic autoimmune disease often appearing in children, where the pancreas fails to produce enough insulin. Unlike the more common type 2 diabetes, T1D cannot be prevented t...

item.title: Lisa Kudrow rewatching 'Friends' as she copes with Matthew Perry's passing
item.description: Lisa Kudrow, well-known for her role as Phoebe Buffay in the iconic TV series 'Friends', has shared that she has been finding comfort in revisiting episodes of the show following the recent passing of...

item.title: Incident near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant kills one amid regional military strikes
item.description: A projectile struck near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing one member of security personnel, according to the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA). Iranian authorities confirmed the incident ...

item.title: Balochistan Government Orders Early Closure of Markets, Restaurants and Wedding Halls
item.description: QUETTA: The Balochistan government has issued new directives for energy conservation across the province. As per the Home Department notification, markets and shopping centres will now close by 8pm da...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV completes all 14 Stations of the Cross at the Colosseum on Good Friday
item.description: On Good Friday, Pope Leo XIV personally carried the wooden cross through all 14 Stations of the Cross at the Colosseum in Rome. This marked the first time in decades that a pope has undertaken the ful...

item.title: Rising Fuel Costs Likely to Push Up Bread Prices in South Africa
item.description: South Africa is experiencing a significant surge in fuel prices, with petrol reaching a four-year high and diesel breaking previous records. This spike is expected to have a direct impact on food pric...

item.title: Study Finds Dust from California’s Drying Salton Sea Slows Children’s Lung Development
item.description: For decades, California’s Salton Sea has been shrinking due to water diversion, drought, and climate change. Once a thriving resort area, it has become a major environmental and public health problem....

item.title: Marisha Matthews Reflects on Meeting Her Partner and Their Journey Together
item.description: In 2014, Marisha Matthews was living in Adelaide with her two children when she encountered Paul, a charismatic minister, through community work supporting refugees. Their initial connection was subtl...
